description.p: AstroBurst is an open-source desktop application for processing astronomical images. Drop in your FITS or ASDF files, compose RGB from narrowband channels, stack with sigma clipping, apply tone curves, and export. Everything runs locally with no cloud dependencies.

Built on Rust for the heavy lifting, React for the interface, and WebGPU for real-time preview. Opens a 2 GB IFU datacube in 300 ms, processes 10 frames at 1.4 GB/s, and renders STF adjustments in 8 ms on GPU.

ul.li: 11-step ComposeWizard pipeline with tone curves and non-destructive processing
FITS and ASDF format support (first non-Python ASDF reader)
Blend presets for narrowband data (SHO, HOO, Foraxx, Hubble Legacy)
Richardson-Lucy deconvolution, wavelet denoising, masked stretch
Star detection, plate solving via astrometry.net, WCS readout
Works with JWST, Hubble, and Roman Space Telescope data

screenshot.caption: NGC 628 RGB composite from 17 JWST NIRCam filters with ComposeWizard
Eagle Nebula HST narrowband with automatic channel mapping
M51 galaxy with spline-based tone curves in AdjustStep
NGC 628 masked stretch with star protection

description.p: Tone curves, auto-STF preview, cache-only pipeline, 39 TypeScript fixes.

ul.li: Spline-based curve editor with per-channel R/G/B and linked RGB
Auto-STF preview for all linear-domain commands
Cache-only intermediate processing (zero disk writes until export)
Windows asset protocol path safety fix

description.p: Non-destructive composite pipeline, STF consistency, blend preset resolver.
